# Table D. Hold-Out Size Sensitivity (Variance Stress Test)

| Setting | Median | IQR | Min / Max | Best Model |
|---------|--------|-----|-----------|------------|
| ANOVA, P=6 | 69.5% | 12.4% | 51.0% / 89.7% | MLP |
| ANOVA, P=2 | 76.6% | 22.0% | 36.1% / 98.3% | MLP |
| PCA, P=6 | 53.2% | 6.9% | 34.4% / 74.1% | KNN |
| PCA, P=2 | 54.2% | 10.0% | 26.0% / 78.5% | KNN |

---

## Footnote

P = held-out subjects per fold. Smaller P=2 increases fold-to-fold variance (wider IQR); P=6 is the primary setting, P=2 is a stress test.

*Generated by `create_table_d_holdout_sensitivity.py`*

---

## Statistical Tests

> All tests use the **best-per-fold** accuracy vector (max HP config per fold).
> Bootstrap CIs use 10,000 resamples, seed=42.
> *Generated by `compute_real_statistics.py` on 2026-03-02*


### P=6 vs P=2 comparison (Mann-Whitney U, unpaired — different fold compositions)

| Feature | Best model P=6 | Median P=6 [95% CI] | Best model P=2 | Median P=2 [95% CI] | Δ median (pp) | 95% CI for Δ | Cliff's δ | Effect | p (two-sided) |
|---------|---------------|---------------------|---------------|---------------------|--------------|--------------|-----------|--------|---------------|
| ANOVA | MLP | 73.1% [72.1%–76.2%] | MLP | 80.4% [76.0%–85.0%] | -7.2 | [-12.2, -1.2] | -0.32 | small | = 0.006 ✅ |
| PCA | KNN | 57.5% [56.0%–60.4%] | KNN | 59.1% [56.4%–63.1%] | -1.6 | [-5.5, +1.7] | -0.17 | small | = 0.151 (n.s.) |

> **Note:** P=2 and P=6 experiments draw from different subject pools (49 vs 65 subjects) and different fold compositions.
> IQR widening at P=2 reflects genuine fold-to-fold instability, not a systematic bias.
